summary: yandex-geobase: dependency-confusion beacon to hardcoded bare IP on postinstall
details: Package name impersonates the Yandex publisher namespace and ships a postinstall.js that performs an unconditional plain-HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare IPv4 endpoint (http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase) at install time, transmitting package name, version, and a campaign identifier. The destination is a non-TLS bare-IP host with no relationship to any Yandex publisher infrastructure, and the URL path encodes a dependency-confusion campaign tag. This is the canonical success-beacon half of a dependency-confusion attack: when an internal build system mistakenly resolves the squatted public name, the beacon confirms reachability and identifies the victim environment to the operator, enabling targeted follow-on attacks against that build pipeline. A README self-label as a benign proof-of-concept does not change the behavior: the install-time callout to attacker-controlled infrastructure executes on every default `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
